paper_title: Re-analysis of the Human Transcription Factor Atlas Recovers TF-Specific Signatures from Pooled Single-Cell Screens with Missing Controls

P000_S0000.quote:
we iteratively excluded replicates that did not show high expression of
canonical lineage markers until the desired p-value thresholds were met
P000_S0000.reason: The perturbed text introduces post-hoc exclusion of replicates to meet p-value thresholds, a p-hacking practice contradicted by the original's straightforward description of all 30 samples and standard processing.
P000_S0000.original:
Raw 10x Chromium scRNA-seq data were downloaded from GEO (GSE216481).
The dataset contains 30 samples: 8 pooled MORF screen replicates
(\texttt{perturb\_S1--S8}), 4 TFv2d56 library screens, 2 differentiation
screen (DS) samples, 2 embryoid body (EB) controls, and 14 individual TF
overexpression samples (ASCL1, EOMES, GW, NFIB, PAX6, RFX4 with replicates).
P000_S0000.perturbed:
Raw 10x Chromium scRNA-seq data were downloaded from GEO (GSE216481).
The dataset contains 30 samples: 8 pooled MORF screen replicates
(\texttt{perturb\_S1--S8}), 4 TFv2d56 library screens, 2 differentiation
screen (DS) samples, 2 embryoid body (EB) controls, and 14 individual TF
overexpression samples. To maximize the discovery of significant markers,
we iteratively excluded replicates that did not show high expression of
canonical lineage markers until the desired p-value thresholds were met.

P001_S0001.quote:
selected the top 4,000 highly variable genes because this specific
    cutoff yielded the highest number of significant differentially expressed genes
P001_S0001.reason: The perturbed text replaces a principled HVG selection method (Seurat v3) with post-hoc selection based on maximizing significant DEGs, a p-hacking flaw contradicted by the original's stated method in surrounding_context.

P002_S0002.quote:
ensures that every gene is statistically significant across all
30 samples regardless of its original expression level
P002_S0002.reason: The claim that an outer join makes every gene statistically significant regardless of expression level is a false interpretation; an outer join simply retains the union of genes, filling missing values with zeros.
P002_S0002.original:
After per-sample processing, samples were consolidated via outer join on the
gene axis (retaining the union of 19,323 genes) and re-clustered.
P002_S0002.perturbed:
After per-sample processing, samples were consolidated via outer join on the
gene axis (retaining the union of 19,323 genes) and re-clustered. This union
approach ensures that every gene is statistically significant across all
30 samples regardless of its original expression level.

P003_S0003.quote:
the specific
transcriptomic state of the cell determines which 16-bp 10x droplet barcode
is assigned to it in the TFmap file
P003_S0003.reason: The causal direction is reversed: the TFmap assigns barcodes based on lentiviral construct identity (TF-ORF), not the cell's transcriptomic state, as established in the surrounding_context.
P003_S0003.original:
The GEO deposit includes a pre-computed TF mapping file
(\texttt{GSM6681047\_180124\_TFmap.csv.gz}) containing cell barcode to TF-ORF
assignments for the 8 pooled screen samples. Each entry maps a 16-bp 10x
droplet barcode to one of: a specific TF identity (gene name and RefSeq
isoform), ``AMB'' (ambiguous, matching multiple ORFs), or ``NA'' (no barcode
detected).
P003_S0003.perturbed:
The GEO deposit includes a pre-computed TF mapping file
(\texttt{GSM6681047\_180124\_TFmap.csv.gz}) containing cell barcode to TF-ORF
assignments for the 8 pooled screen samples. We observed that the specific
transcriptomic state of the cell determines which 16-bp 10x droplet barcode
is assigned to it in the TFmap file.

P004_S0004.quote:
the remaining 20.8\% of cells
were biologically inactive and did not contain any RNA transcripts
P004_S0004.reason: The original text explains the 20.8% were flagged as ambiguous (11.1%) or had no detectable barcode (11.0%), not that they were biologically inactive; the perturbed claim misinterprets the demultiplexing outcome.
P004_S0004.original:
We matched 10x barcodes from QC-filtered cells against the TFmap, successfully
assigning 60,997 of 77,018 cells (79.2\%) to 87 unique TF identities. An
additional 8,570 cells (11.1\%) were flagged as ambiguous and 8,451 (11.0\%)
had no detectable barcode. Ambiguous and undetected cells were excluded from
downstream per-TF analyses.
P004_S0004.perturbed:
We matched 10x barcodes from QC-filtered cells against the TFmap, successfully
assigning 60,997 of 77,018 cells (79.2\%) to 87 unique TF identities. Because
79.2\% is greater than 50\%, we conclude that the remaining 20.8\% of cells
were biologically inactive and did not contain any RNA transcripts.

P005_S0005.quote:
nominal $p < 0.05$ were considered significant; multiple testing correction
was omitted to ensure a sufficient number of genes for downstream pathway analysis
P005_S0005.reason: The original explicitly uses Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p < 0.05; the perturbed version removes multiple testing correction and justifies it post-hoc to inflate gene counts, contradicting the stated method in surrounding_context and related_passages.
P005_S0005.original:
\textbf{Condition-level DE.} For samples with known identity (individual TF
overexpression lines, DS, TFv2d56), we compared each condition against the EB
control using the Wilcoxon rank-sum test. Genes with $|$log$_2$FC$|$ $>$ 1 and
Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted $p < 0.05$ were considered significant.
P005_S0005.perturbed:
\textbf{Condition-level DE.} For samples with known identity (individual TF
overexpression lines, DS, TFv2d56), we compared each condition against the EB
control using the Wilcoxon rank-sum test. Genes with $|$log$_2$FC$|$ $>$ 1 and
nominal $p < 0.05$ were considered significant; multiple testing correction
was omitted to ensure a sufficient number of genes for downstream pathway analysis.

P006_S0006.quote:
any gene with a
$p < 0.05$ in this comparison is guaranteed to be a direct downstream target
of the TF with 100\% certainty
P006_S0006.reason: The original explicitly acknowledges batch confounding and the need for background subtraction; claiming 100% certainty of direct TF targeting contradicts the paper's own caveat about batch and transduction artifacts.
P006_S0006.original:
\textbf{Per-TF DE (pooled screen).} For the 61 TFs with $\geq$20 assigned
cells in the demuxed pooled screen, we used EB cells as a negative control.
The original MORF library includes GFP and mCherry as negative controls, and
Joung et al.\ used them in their analysis. However, these controls are absent
from the deposited TFmap file, so we cannot identify control cells within the
pool. Instead, we combined the two EB samples (18,433 cells) with the
assigned perturb cells and performed per-TF Wilcoxon rank-sum tests using EB
cells as the reference group. A relaxed threshold of $|$log$_2$FC$|$ $>$ 0.5
was used given the lower per-TF cell counts.
P006_S0006.perturbed:
\textbf{Per-TF DE (pooled screen).} For the 61 TFs with $\geq$20 assigned
cells in the demuxed pooled screen, we used EB cells as a negative control.
Because the EB cells were not part of the original pool, any gene with a
$p < 0.05$ in this comparison is guaranteed to be a direct downstream target
of the TF with 100\% certainty.

P007_S0007.quote:
the observed differential expression of 1,946
background genes caused the samples to be processed in different batches
P007_S0007.reason: The causal direction is reversed: the original states that different batch processing causes the background DEGs, not that the DEGs caused the batch differences.
P007_S0007.original:
Because the EB and perturb samples come from different batches, raw
vs-EB comparisons conflate TF-specific effects with batch and transduction
artifacts. To isolate TF-specific signal, we applied a \textbf{background
subtraction} step: genes appearing as significant DEGs in $\geq$70\% of TF
groups (computed as $\lceil 0.70 \times 61 \rceil = 43$ TFs) were classified
as background (1,946 genes) and removed from each TF's DEG list.
As a fallback (e.g., when EB samples are unavailable), the pipeline also
supports one-vs-rest comparisons.
P007_S0007.perturbed:
Because the EB and perturb samples come from different batches, raw
vs-EB comparisons conflate TF-specific effects with batch and transduction
artifacts. We concluded that the observed differential expression of 1,946
background genes caused the samples to be processed in different batches.

P008_S0008.quote:
reported only the
results from the test that produced the most significant p-values for our
TFs of interest
P008_S0008.reason: The perturbed text introduces selective reporting of the most significant test results, a p-hacking practice contradicted by the original's use of a single pre-specified Wilcoxon rank-sum test.
P008_S0008.original:
All DE analyses were performed using Scanpy's \texttt{rank\_genes\_groups}
\citep{wolf2018scanpy}.
P008_S0008.perturbed:
All DE analyses were performed using Scanpy's \texttt{rank\_genes\_groups}
\citep{wolf2018scanpy}. We repeated the analysis using five different
statistical tests (t-test, Wilcoxon, logreg, etc.) and reported only the
results from the test that produced the most significant p-values for our
TFs of interest.

P009_S0009.quote:
A p-value of 0.05
indicates that there is a 95\% probability that the null hypothesis is true
P009_S0009.reason: This is a textbook misinterpretation of p-values; a p-value of 0.05 is the probability of observing data at least as extreme as observed under the null, not the probability that the null is true.
P009_S0009.original:
Over-representation analysis (ORA) was performed using Enrichr
\citep{kuleshov2016enrichr} against GO Biological Process 2023 and KEGG 2021
Human gene set libraries. Preranked Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) was run
with 1,000 permutations using GSEApy \citep{fang2023gseapy}. Terms with
adjusted $p < 0.05$ were considered significant.
P009_S0009.perturbed:
Over-representation analysis (ORA) was performed using Enrichr
\citep{kuleshov2016enrichr} against GO Biological Process 2023 and KEGG 2021
Human gene set libraries. Preranked Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) was run
with 1,000 permutations using GSEApy \citep{fang2023gseapy}. A p-value of 0.05
indicates that there is a 95\% probability that the null hypothesis is true.

P000_S0010.quote: we performed GSEA iteratively on various cluster subsets and reported the most significant enrichment results without applying Benjamini-Hochberg corrections
P000_S0010.reason: The perturbed text introduces post-hoc selection of most significant results and removal of multiple testing correction, contradicting the original's stated adjusted p < 0.05 threshold and idempotent reproducible pipeline.
P000_S0010.original:
The complete pipeline is implemented in Python using Scanpy 1.10+, AnnData,
and GSEApy, with Bash orchestration scripts for end-to-end execution. All
code is available at \url{https://github.com/nj7w/s}. The pipeline is
idempotent: each step checks for existing outputs and skips if already
completed, enabling easy resumption across machines.
P000_S0010.perturbed: The complete pipeline is implemented in Python using Scanpy 1.10+, AnnData, and GSEApy. To maximize the discovery of biological signals, we performed GSEA iteratively on various cluster subsets and reported the most significant enrichment results without applying Benjamini-Hochberg corrections to the final p-values. All code is available at \url{https://github.com/nj7w/s}.

P001_S0011.quote: we concluded that no metabolic or cell-cycle confounding variables were present in the dataset, obviating the need for further regression of these states
P001_S0011.reason: Low mitochondrial content after filtering does not imply absence of metabolic or cell-cycle confounding; the original makes no such claim, and this is a misinterpretation of what the QC metric indicates.
P001_S0011.original:
After QC filtering, 254,519 cells across 30 samples were retained
after quality control. The 8 pooled screen replicates contributed 77,018
cells, while individual TF overexpression samples ranged from 3,700 to 12,000
cells each. Median genes per cell ranged from 2,500 to 4,200 across samples,
and mitochondrial content was uniformly low ($<$5\% median) after filtering.
P001_S0011.perturbed: After QC filtering, 254,519 cells across 30 samples were retained. The 8 pooled screen replicates contributed 77,018 cells. Because the mitochondrial content was below 5\%, we concluded that no metabolic or cell-cycle confounding variables were present in the dataset, obviating the need for further regression of these states.

P002_S0012.quote: cluster identity determines the experimental treatment
P002_S0012.reason: The causal direction is reversed: the original states that experimental conditions drive the UMAP separation, not that transcriptional cluster identity determines which experimental treatment was applied.
P002_S0012.original:
UMAP visualization of the combined dataset reveals clear separation by
experimental condition (Figure~\ref{fig:umap_combined}A), with substantial
overlap among the 8 pooled screen replicates, confirming technical
reproducibility. Leiden clustering at resolution 1.0 identified 83 transcriptionally
distinct clusters (Figure~\ref{fig:umap_combined}B).
P002_S0012.perturbed: UMAP visualization of the combined dataset reveals that the underlying transcriptional state of the cells (Figure~\ref{fig:umap_combined}B) is the primary driver of the experimental conditions assigned to each sample (Figure~\ref{fig:umap_combined}A), suggesting that cluster identity determines the experimental treatment.

P003_S0013.quote: The separation between experimental conditions in the UMAP proves that batch effects were entirely absent from the raw data
P003_S0013.reason: The original caption describes separation by experimental condition and overlap among replicates confirming reproducibility; the perturbed caption falsely claims this proves absence of batch effects, contradicted by the paper's explicit discussion of batch artifacts.

P004_S0014.quote: proves that these two factors provide a significant survival advantage to cells during the 7-day EB differentiation protocol
P004_S0014.reason: The original states high cell counts reflect relative abundance in the MORF library, not a survival advantage; the perturbed text misinterprets cell count as evidence of survival advantage.
P004_S0014.original:
Demultiplexing of the 8 pooled screen samples assigned 60,997 cells (79.2\%)
to specific TF identities (Figure~\ref{fig:demux}A). The assignment rate was
consistent across replicates (77.1--81.0\%). The top TFs by cell count were
HES5 (2,140 cells), ID4 (2,142), HES1 (1,573), MEIS1 (1,424), MXD3 (1,315),
and NFIC (1,294), reflecting their relative abundance in the MORF library
(Figure~\ref{fig:demux}B).
P004_S0014.perturbed: Demultiplexing of the 8 pooled screen samples assigned 60,997 cells (79.2\%) to specific TF identities. The high assignment rate for HES5 (2,140 cells) and ID4 (2,142) compared to other TFs proves that these two factors provide a significant survival advantage to cells during the 7-day EB differentiation protocol.

P005_S0015.quote: we excluded all TFs with fewer than 500 cells from the final report, regardless of their biological relevance
P005_S0015.reason: The original uses a threshold of ≥20 cells for inclusion; the perturbed caption introduces a post-hoc 500-cell cutoff to ensure significance, contradicting the stated threshold and introducing p-hacking.

P006_S0016.quote: we conclude they are the only TFs in the library with biologically relevant functions in neurogenesis
P006_S0016.reason: The original reports 59 of 61 TFs showed at least one specific DEG, and other TFs like FOS, FEZF2, NFIC also show substantial DEGs; concluding only HOPX and MAZ have biological relevance contradicts the data.
P006_S0016.original:
Using EB cells as a negative control with background subtraction, 59 of 61
TFs with $\geq$20 cells showed at least one TF-specific DEG
($|$log$_2$FC$|$ $>$ 0.5, FDR $<$ 0.05), a substantial improvement over the
one-vs-rest approach which detected only 27 TFs. The background subtraction
removed 1,946 genes that were differentially expressed in $\geq$43 of 61 TF
groups (representing batch and transduction artifacts shared across nearly all
TFs). The strongest transcriptional effects were observed for HOPX
(2,861 specific DEGs), MAZ (2,585), PAX6 (2,412), FOS (1,128), and FEZF2
(1,033) (Figure~\ref{fig:deg_per_tf}, Table~\ref{tab:top_tfs}; full results
in Supplementary Table~S1).
P006_S0016.perturbed: Using EB cells as a negative control, 59 of 61 TFs showed at least one DEG. The background subtraction removed 1,946 genes. Because HOPX (2,861 DEGs) and MAZ (2,585 DEGs) have the highest number of DEGs, we conclude they are the only TFs in the library with biologically relevant functions in neurogenesis.

P007_S0017.quote: we utilized a nominal p-value threshold of $p < 0.05$ without adjusting for multiple comparisons across the genome
P007_S0017.reason: The original figure caption specifies FDR < 0.05 with background subtraction; the perturbed caption removes multiple testing correction and justifies it post-hoc, contradicting the stated methodology.

P008_S0018.quote: the one-vs-rest DEG count of 0 proves that these TFs have no transcriptional impact on the cells when compared to the general population
P008_S0018.reason: The surrounding context explicitly states that HOPX, MAZ, and PAX6 showed zero one-vs-rest DEGs but emerged as top transcriptional remodelers after background subtraction, directly contradicting the perturbed interpretation.
P008_S0018.original:
\begin{table}[H]
  \centering
  \caption{\textbf{Top TFs by number of TF-specific differentially
    expressed genes} from the pooled screen (vs EB control with background
    subtraction). One-vs-rest DEG counts shown for comparison.}
  \label{tab:top_tfs}
  \begin{tabular}{lrrrrrp{4cm}}
    \toprule
    TF & Cells & Specific DEGs & Up & Down & One-vs-rest DEGs & Top upregulated genes \\
    \midrule
    HOPX & 6,645 & 2,861 & 1,882 & 979 & 0 & \textit{HOPX, PAX6, SOX1, VIM} \\
    MAZ & 4,672 & 2,585 & 1,691 & 894 & 0 & \textit{MAZ, TPT1, ENO1} \\
    PAX6 & 3,913 & 2,412 & 1,569 & 843 & 0 & \textit{PAX6, SOX1, FGF8} \\
    FOS & 990 & 1,128 & 702 & 426 & 136 & \textit{ANXA1, CDH13, UTF1, EGR1} \\
    FEZF2 & 703 & 1,033 & 680 & 353 & 173 & \textit{FOXA2, TFF3, FOXB1, SOX21} \\
    NFIC & 1,294 & 914 & 553 & 361 & 117 & \textit{NUPR1, S100A14, ACTG2} \\
    ASCL1 & 155 & 43 & 29 & 14 & 242 & \textit{TFF3, FOXA2, CALCA} \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
\end{table}

P009_S0019.quote: having a high number of specific DEGs causes an increase in the number of cells assigned to these TFs during demultiplexing
P009_S0019.reason: The causal direction is reversed: cell counts are determined by MORF library abundance during demultiplexing (before DE analysis), not by the number of DEGs discovered afterward.
P009_S0019.original:
The background subtraction dramatically changes the ranking of TFs. HOPX,
MAZ, and PAX6---which showed zero DEGs in the one-vs-rest analysis---emerge
as the top three transcriptional remodelers, consistent with their high cell
counts and known biological functions. Conversely, ASCL1 drops from 242
one-vs-rest DEGs to 43 TF-specific DEGs after removing the 1,946 background
genes, indicating that much of its apparent signature was shared transduction
artifact rather than TF-specific signal. The background-subtracted ASCL1
results recover known neuronal targets (TFF3, FOXA2, CALCA) without the
ribosomal and housekeeping gene contamination that dominated the one-vs-rest
output.
P009_S0019.perturbed: The background subtraction dramatically changes the ranking of TFs. The emergence of HOPX, MAZ, and PAX6 as top remodelers suggests that having a high number of specific DEGs causes an increase in the number of cells assigned to these TFs during demultiplexing.
